file_insights.purpose: Initial database migration for customers app
Initial database migration for media app
Initial database migration for notifications app

patterns_discovered.description: All models use uuid.uuid4 as primary key instead of auto-increment integers
patterns_discovered.benefit: Better for distributed systems and privacy

patterns_discovered.description: Standard created_at, updated_at, and deleted_at (for soft delete) fields on models
ForeignKey to core.organization on all major models for multi-tenancy
Customer model includes denormalized fields (total_orders, total_spent, loyalty_points_balance)
patterns_discovered.rationale: Performance optimization for frequently accessed metrics

patterns_discovered.description: Models use JSONField for flexible, unstructured metadata storage
CharField with predefined choices for statuses and categorization
Multiple multi-field indexes for query optimization
patterns_discovered.occurrences: 12+ indexes across Customer and CustomerVisit models
patterns_discovered.strategy: index on (organization, field) for tenant-specific queries

gotchas_discovered.description: Customer model has unique_together on (organization, email) but email is nullable. In most databases, NULL != NULL, so multiple records can have NULL email without violating constraint.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Either make email non-nullable or use a partial unique index that filters out NULLs

gotchas_discovered.description: total_orders, total_spent, and loyalty_points_balance on Customer must be kept in sync with actual data. No database-level enforcement.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Implement application-level triggers or careful transaction management to maintain consistency

gotchas_discovered.description: All customers, media, and notifications migrations depend on core.0001_initial. If core migration fails, these will fail.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Ensure core migration is always applied first; consider dependency management in CI/CD

gotchas_discovered.description: deleted_at field is indexed and used for filtering, but soft-deleted records still consume space and appear in unfiltered queries.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Implement queryset mixin that filters deleted_at=None by default to prevent accidental data leaks

gotchas_discovered.description: UUIDField with editable=False and default=uuid.uuid4 - uuid4 is called at import time, not per-instance, which could cause issues.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Verify that uuid.uuid4 is being called per-instance (it should be via callable default) and not once at import

gotchas_discovered.description: Feedback.customer is nullable (for anonymous feedback) but many customer-related fields in other models are required, creating data modeling inconsistency.
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Document when customer can be null and ensure application code handles null customer cases

approach_outcome.description: Successfully generated and applied migrations for customers, media, and notifications apps
approach_outcome.approach_used: Django migration generation via makemigrations or manual migration creation
approach_outcome.relationships_established: multiple ForeignKey relationships with CASCADE and SET_NULL delete strategies
data_integrity_measures: soft delete support via deleted_at field
approach_outcome.challenges_overcome: Successfully created complex migration with multiple models, relationships, and indexes across 3 apps

recommendations.recommendation: Implement database-level triggers or application-level signals to keep denormalized Customer metrics (total_orders, total_spent) synchronized with actual data
recommendations.rationale: Prevents data inconsistency and reporting errors

recommendations.recommendation: Create a base model mixin or abstract model that includes audit fields (created_at, updated_at, deleted_at) to reduce duplication
recommendations.rationale: DRY principle and easier maintenance across models

recommendations.recommendation: Add database query analysis tools/APM to monitor performance of multi-field indexes, especially (organization, field) patterns
recommendations.rationale: Ensure indexes are being used effectively and identify missing indexes as query patterns evolve

recommendations.recommendation: Consider creating a QuerySet custom manager with soft delete filtering built-in to prevent accidental queries of deleted records
recommendations.rationale: Reduces bugs from accidentally exposing soft-deleted data

recommendations.recommendation: Document the multi-tenancy pattern with organization ForeignKey and any assumptions about data isolation
recommendations.rationale: Ensures developers understand data scoping rules and security implications

recommendations.recommendation: Create factory fixtures for each model with proper relationships for integration testing
recommendations.rationale: Simplifies testing of multi-model interactions and migrations

recommendations.recommendation: Monitor CustomerVisit table growth rate and consider archiving/partitioning strategy for high-volume scenarios
recommendations.rationale: Visit records can accumulate quickly and impact query performance over time
